Impact: external stakeholders using saved permalinks from scheduled reports get 404s and cannot access traces referenced in alerts. Environment: prod us-east during a retention backfill window. Affects scheduled report links shared externally.

title: Speculative tool-schema hydration causing delayed streamed chunk delivery and mid-stream timeouts
preview:
Issue summary: Customer reports sharp increase in end-to-end latency for streamed responses when prompts include tool calls. The stream starts but streamed chunks are delayed ~2-8s mid-response, occasionally leading to client-side timeouts.

Impact: Affects multiple production customers (enterprise tier). Intermitte...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reports sharp increase in end-to-end latency for streamed responses when prompts include tool calls. The stream starts but streamed chunks are delayed ~2-8s mid-response, occasionally leading to client-side timeouts.

Impact: Affects multiple production customers (enterprise tier). Intermittent but reproducible with tool call enabled; increases tail latency and causes streaming sessions to abort after client-side timeouts.

Environment: prod cluster in us-east, streaming route /v1/chat/stream with toolcall preflight enabled and schema-hydration flag set to speculative=true. Observed for rw-open-7b-v1 and rw-llama-13b-v2.

Observed behavior: Initial tokens stream normally for ~200-400ms, then a stall of 2-8s while server emits heartbeats but no content chunks. Traces show a synchronous preflight/schema hydration step invoked mid-stream for tool metadata.

Expect...

Issue summary:

text: Issue summary: During PulseRx's morning ingest window (09:30-10:15 UTC, 2026-03-11) a subset of streaming completions returned interleaved token sequences and occasionally duplicated segments from concurrent requests. This produced garbled patient-facing summaries and inflated token counts on billing. Impact: high — customer-facing clinical summarization pipeline produced invalid notes for ~0.9% of requests in the window, and PulseRx reports a ~22% increase in billable tokens for the same period.

text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 504 / request timeouts for single generation requests when using very large max_tokens (typically >= 120k token window). Failures occur late in the generation (after ~70-95% of requested tokens produced). Suspected stall during KV-cache refill / eviction cycle in serving runtime when prefill and decode phases overlap under heavy batching.

Impact:
- Affects multiple request attempts from NimbusAI production workloads on Dedicated pools in us-east; blocking their long-transcript generation features.
- Symptoms: spike in 5xx (504 gateway timeouts) and client-observed disconnects; partial outputs received before termination.

Environment:
- Dedicated customer pool (dedicated), prod:us-east; models: gptx-16b-fp16 primarily.
- Requests routed through API gateway with default 60s client timeout in their code, but server-side timeouts observed bey...

title: Mid-cycle seat add led to prorated surcharge removing negotiated volume discount on consolidated invoice

text:
Issue summary:
Customer SiftMail reports a charge on their March consolidated invoice that appears to negate their negotiated volume discount after adding 12 seats mid-cycle. The invoice shows a prorated "surcharge" line where per-seat pricing reverted to list price for the prorated portion rather than applying the customer's tiered volume discount.

Impact:
- Customer sees a $4,322 unexpected line item affecting month close and internal chargebacks.
- Customer concerned this will recur on future mid-cycle seat changes.

Environment:
- Enterprise org on reserved/dedicated plan with negotiated tier: 31+ seats => $1.80/seat; 11-30 seats => $2.10/seat.
- New seats added 2026-03-05T15:40:12Z (12 seats) via admin console. Billing cycle starts 1st of month.

Requested outcome:
- Clarify why prorate used list price for added seats instead of applying the negotiated tier once total seat count...

text:
Issue summary:
Customer LexiconSoft reports elevated 502/503 errors and failed embedding upload jobs when sending large batched embedding payloads (~50–200MB) using our REST API over HTTP/2 with chunked transfer encoding. Errors began 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC after a gradual traffic increase.

Impact:
- Several customer embedding jobs failed (25% failure rate on a 40-job run).
- Increased API error-rate across their tenant (~P50->P95 error increase).
- Affects production P95 latency and caused retry storms from client side.

Environment:
- prod cluster in us-east-1; customer on enterprise plan with Dedicated routing to high-priority pool.
- Requests use HTTP/2 with chunked transfer-encoding and per-request streaming.
- Model: text-embedding-3-large pinned for this customer.

Observed behavior and tentative root cause hypothesis:

title: Concurrent client retries split transaction boundary causing duplicate tool executions across Python and Go SDKs
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports duplicated external side effects (webhook POST to order processing) when their app issues concurrent retries from both Python (2.4.1) and Go (1.7.0) SDK clients in a short window. Impact: Duplicate orders reaching downstream systems, billing exposure, and customer trust concern. Obser...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports duplicated external side effects (webhook POST to order processing) when their app issues concurrent retries from both Python (2.4.1) and Go (1.7.0) SDK clients in a short window. Impact: Duplicate orders reaching downstream systems, billing exposure, and customer trust concern. Observed behavior: Under burst failure conditions the orchestrator appears to split what the client treats as a single transactional interaction into two distinct server-side request groups, and two tool invocations are delivered to the downstream webhook. Expected behavior: A single external tool call (webhook) per logical user action even if the client retries locally; SDK/platform should provide clear idempotency guarantee guidance and mechanisms to prevent duplicate side-effects.

title: TCP timestamp stripping by corporate firewall causes intermittent gRPC reconnects and degraded streaming in private deployment
preview:

text:
Issue summary:
Customers on a private VPC deployment intermittently observe gRPC stream resets and frequent reconnects when sending long-lived streaming inference requests. Impact: streaming clients experience token duplication, reconnect churn, and increased tail latency, causing transient user-facing failures during payments reconciliation jobs.

- Tail latency increases from 120ms median to >1.5s p95 for streaming responses.

Environment:
- Deployment: Private (customer-managed VPC peering + egress through corporate firewall)
- Region: us-east-1 (customer-routed egress)
- Redwood runtime: serving-runtime v2026.02.10
- OBS: TLS stays up; application-level gRPC shows RST and transient EOFs.

Issue summary:

prod/us-west, dedicated pool for LexiDocs. Session-sidecars (Python 3.11) present on each node to manage streaming sessions and multipart uploads. Serving runtime (Rust) remained mostly healthy on GPU hosts.

title: Intermittent 429 bursts for multiplexed websocket chat causing degraded UX
preview: Issue summary: NimbusHealth is seeing intermittent bursts of HTTP 429 responses returned to multiplexed websocket clients on the hosted API during peak business hours. Impact: Users in their clinical triage chat are experiencing message failures and UI retries, increasing perceived latency and leading to dropped con...
text: Issue summary: NimbusHealth is seeing intermittent bursts of HTTP 429 responses returned to multiplexed websocket clients on the hosted API during peak business hours. Impact: Users in their clinical triage chat are experiencing message failures and UI retries, increasing perceived latency and leading to dropped conversations. Scope: Affects ~10 client connection pools from NimbusHealth (enterprise account) in us-east region; seen across redwood-chat-small and medium models. Expected: Multiplexed websocket connections should see smoothing by token-level and connection-level rate limits with transparent backpressure, not sudden 429 bursts. Observed: Spikes of 429s lasting 20-40s every ~15 minutes, correlating with bursty fan-out from a single internal service. Steps to reproduce: 1) Establish multiplexed websocket session using shared API key for multiple browser clients (as customer i...

title: Configuring sustained vs burst quota for multiplexed ingestion workers to avoid 429s
preview:
Issue summary: Customer Arcadia Labs reports recurring 429 throttles during steady-state ingestion runs from a fleet of short-lived uploader workers. They see 429s even when token usage looks within expected monthly/rolling quotas.

Impact: Intermittent failed uploads (10-25% of requests during 1–2 hour ingestion wi...
text:
Issue summary: Customer Arcadia Labs reports recurring 429 throttles during steady-state ingestion runs from a fleet of short-lived uploader workers. They see 429s even when token usage looks within expected monthly/rolling quotas.

Impact: Intermittent failed uploads (10-25% of requests during 1–2 hour ingestion windows), delays in downstream indexing, and elevated retry traffic causing secondary waves of throttles. No billing disputes opened, but customer is concerned about reliability for nightly syncs.

Environment: self_serve tenant in prod (us-east primary, eu-west failover). Workers spawn ephemeral API keys (lifetime ~5–10 minutes) and open 20–50 concurrent connections per worker process. Ingestion pattern: steady sustained throughput ~120 requests/s for 90–120 minutes, with microbursts when workers restart.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create N ephemeral keys and fan out 300 worker...

title: Edge probe split causes half-formed streamed messages after reconnect
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that after an intermittent network disconnect, SSE streams resume but the first resumed payloads are half-formed JSON fragments and the client parser fails. Impact: Several NexaHealth production workflows ingesting streamed clinical summaries are producing invalid partial objects; cus...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports that after an intermittent network disconnect, SSE streams resume but the first resumed payloads are half-formed JSON fragments and the client parser fails. Impact: Several NexaHealth production workflows ingesting streamed clinical summaries are producing invalid partial objects; customer reports ~15% of resumed sessions encounter parse errors and drops. Environment: prod, us-east, edge-proxy path through third-party CDN probe that occasionally inserts synthetic TCP probe packets. Observed behavior: On reconnect the server sends a resumed chunk that starts mid-UTF8 sequence and omits the JSON array opener; client logs "JSON parse error: unexpected token". Expected behavior: Stream resume should deliver complete, well-delimited framing and finalization events so clients can stitch without parse errors. Business impact: High — downstream ingestion pipeli...

title: Ephemeral port aliasing across multi-NAT egress causes TLS SNI mismatch and intermittent presigned artifact 403s
preview: DatumStream private deployment (VPCE-enabled) started seeing intermittent 403s from presigned S3 URLs used by the serving runtime to stream model shards. The errors are not consistent per-shard; same shard will sometimes succeed and sometimes return 403s for the same task. Client-side retries succeed most of the tim...
text: DatumStream private deployment (VPCE-enabled) started seeing intermittent 403s from presigned S3 URLs used by the serving runtime to stream model shards. The errors are not consistent per-shard; same shard will sometimes succeed and sometimes return 403s for the same task. Client-side retries succeed most of the time. Customer observed increased 5xxs and request retries cascading into throttling-related symptoms.

Issue summary:
Customer experiences truncated SSE streams when TLS 1.3 0-RTT (early data) is used and the client path migrates across edge nodes. The truncation consistently removes the closing markdown code fence (```), and drops the final 1-3 list items from enumerations. Impact:

- Affects user-facing web previews; breaks downstream parsers that expect closed code blocks.

- Network: client mobile app on LTE -> public mobile NAT -> CDN edge -> Redwood edge proxy -> serving pool
Steps to reproduce (customer-provided):

preview:

text:
Issue summary:
Scanly Labs reports a sharp, sustained drop in throughput from their dedicated GPU pool in us-west starting 2026-03-11 09:12 UTC. Latency for streaming generation requests increases and many requests time out. The Redwood autoscaler began oscillating (rapid scale-up then scale-down) around the same time, making the throughput degradation persistent.

Impact:
- Production traffic for Scanly Labs' inference endpoints degraded: 60-80% drop in token throughput on pinned routes.
- Errors observed: tail latency spikes >10s, client-side timeouts, and a small increase in 5xx from serving-runtime.
- Customer-facing feature (real-time report generation) impacted for ~80% of requests routed to dedicated pool.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: scanly-prod-pool-usw (reserved 12 GPUs across 3 nodes)
- Region: us-west
- Models: gptj-compat-13b (primary), redwood-rlhf-7b (fallback)

title: Streaming completions time out (504) when client-side HTTP/2 WINDOW_UPDATE is not sent by corporate proxy
preview:

Customer AcmeFin reports repeated streaming completions from our hosted API returning 504s after long tails of silence. This appears only for some users behind a corporate network appliance.

text:
Issue summary:
Customer AcmeFin reports repeated streaming completions from our hosted API returning 504s after long tails of silence. This appears only for some users behind a corporate network appliance.

Impact:
- ~30 requests/hr from AcmeFin experience 40-120s stalls then 504s when generating long completions (>512 tokens).
- End-user UX broken for interactive flows.
- Customer is enterprise tier; impact threatens a contract renewal conversation.

Environment:
- Prod edge (us-east) -> API Gateway (HTTP/2 enabled) -> NGINX sidecars -> serving-runtime GPU pool.
- Observed on models: gpt-open-13b, redwood-rl-7b.

Steps to reproduce (approx):
1) Customer client opens HTTP/2 connection to /v1/stream with streaming=true.
2) Server starts emitting many small DATA frames (KV-warmup + prefix output) over the stream for ~30-90s.
3) Corporate proxy between client and our edge silently fails ...

Impact: multiple clients seeing ~15-30% failed requests (5xx) for embedding endpoints; customer observed timeouts and client-side retry storms. 3) After sustained churn (~3–5 minutes), upstream serving-runtime processes restart (watchdog), causing 502/503/504 depending on stage.

title: Intermittent tail-drain timeouts for very large max_tokens / long-prefix streaming requests
preview:
Issue summary:
Customers are observing intermittent streaming disconnects and finalization timeouts when submitting requests with very large max_tokens (>=150k) and extended conversation history (many system/user messages). The generation runs for tens of seconds and then the client receives a 504 or streaming conne...
text:
Issue summary:
Customers are observing intermittent streaming disconnects and finalization timeouts when submitting requests with very large max_tokens (>=150k) and extended conversation history (many system/user messages). The generation runs for tens of seconds and then the client receives a 504 or streaming connection is closed without final chunk. This appears to correlate with sustained long-token drains and sudden KV cache thrashing on Dedicated pools.

Impact:
- Affected customer (NoteChat Inc., dedicated) reports ~10% of long-run streaming completions fail to finalize, causing truncated transcripts and user-visible errors.
- Business impact: production feature degradation for premium customers; customers are throttling long-context usage.

Environment:
- Dedicated GPU pool (g4dn-like), prod-us-east, isolated tenant.
- Requests routed to serving runtime version v1.48.0 with bat...

title: Burst of 5xx (mostly 503/502) traced to runtime threadpool starvation from hedged streaming/prefetch traffic
preview: Issue summary: Starting ~2026-03-12 20:15 UTC we observed a sustained uptick in 5xx errors (predominantly 503 and 502) for a Dedicated customer (BrightWrite Inc.) in us-east. Errors correlate with a heavy number of long-lived streaming sessions that use hedged prefetch requests. Impact: API calls to generation/chat ...
text: Issue summary: Starting ~2026-03-12 20:15 UTC we observed a sustained uptick in 5xx errors (predominantly 503 and 502) for a Dedicated customer (BrightWrite Inc.) in us-east. Errors correlate with a heavy number of long-lived streaming sessions that use hedged prefetch requests. Impact: API calls to generation/chat endpoints intermittently return 502/503; streaming sessions abruptly close; customers report degraded latency and dropped responses. Initial hypothesis: runtime threadpool exhaustion / blocking IO in prefetch path leading to upstream failures at the API gateway.

title: Repeat-prompt latency spike (2-3x) after KV shard-mapping rollout; increased cold reads for prefix lookups
preview: Customer reports 2-3x higher tail latencies for repeated prompt requests in long-running chat sessions starting after 2026-03-09 04:00 UTC. Symptoms match cold KV/prefix lookups: prefix cache hit-rate dropped sharply leading to synchronous remote reads and request timeouts (504) for a subset of requests. Impact: Sev...
text: Customer reports 2-3x higher tail latencies for repeated prompt requests in long-running chat sessions starting after 2026-03-09 04:00 UTC. Symptoms match cold KV/prefix lookups: prefix cache hit-rate dropped sharply leading to synchronous remote reads and request timeouts (504) for a subset of requests. Impact: Several hundred requests/minute for the customer's production org experienced >1s extra latency and intermittent timeouts; affecting their chat product's UX. The customer is on an enterprise Dedicated plan and is running production traffic through our us-east cluster where a KV shard-mapping change was rolled out in the morning of 2026-03-09.

title: Catalog sync drift caused wrong model routing, quality regression, and enterprise customer escalation
preview:
Issue summary:
On 2026-03-10 at ~09:23 UTC we observed a large increase in customer-reported hallucinations and unrelated completions for Fresco Retail. Root cause traced to catalog-sync drift: the model-catalog service had an out-of-sync cache window and continued advertising an older (incompatible) variant for the...
text:
Issue summary:
On 2026-03-10 at ~09:23 UTC we observed a large increase in customer-reported hallucinations and unrelated completions for Fresco Retail. Root cause traced to catalog-sync drift: the model-catalog service had an out-of-sync cache window and continued advertising an older (incompatible) variant for the 'product-description' route, causing requests to be routed to gptx-13b-v2-quant instead of the intended gptx-13b-v2 (non-quantized). This introduced repeated generation artifacts and a measurable quality regression for Fresco's production traffic.

Impact:
- Fresco Retail (enterprise) experienced elevated hallucination rate and semantic errors across catalog-enrichment requests for ~2 hours.
- API response quality SLO degraded (precision drop) and several customers opened escalations with support and account team.
- Billing exposure: some requests were served by a higher-c...

text:
Issue summary: Customer BeaconMaps seeing recurring 429s during daily bulk index refresh and low-latency routing for map tile annotations. Impact: Intermittent 429s causing failed embedders and delayed annotations in production (~18% of index refresh jobs fail).

Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated-beacon-3), prod us-west. Heavy mixed workload: scheduled bulk embeddings (multipart uploads) + real-time annotation calls from mobile clients.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Trigger nightly bulk ingest for 80k documents via multipart upload with 16 parallel workers.
2) While ingest runs, send ~200 low-latency chat/annotation requests per second pinned to the same model variant.
3) Observe rate-limit headers and return codes.

Observed behavior: Throttles (429) occur predominantly on multipart upload endpoints and the streaming annotation calls report Retry-After of 5-7s. Customer reports that r...

title: Dedicated capacity pulse: sudden p50/p99 latency increases correlated with misaligned sidecar heartbeats
preview: Issue summary: Customer Lumina Analytics reported a recurring, short-lived pulse in p50 and p99 latency across their dedicated pool starting 2026-03-09 14:00 UTC. Impact: user-facing API calls (generation and streaming) saw p50 increase from ~120ms to ~320ms and p99 spikes to 1.2s for 10-30s windows, causing degrade...
text: Issue summary: Customer Lumina Analytics reported a recurring, short-lived pulse in p50 and p99 latency across their dedicated pool starting 2026-03-09 14:00 UTC. Impact: user-facing API calls (generation and streaming) saw p50 increase from ~120ms to ~320ms and p99 spikes to 1.2s for 10-30s windows, causing degraded interactive experience. Pattern: pulses occur roughly every 5-12 minutes and last ~10-30s; throughput (tokens/s) drops by 20-40% during pulses. Initial hypothesis: interference from background sidecar telemetry/metrics emitters causing transient scheduler/attach delays and autoscaler sensing noise.

title: Persistent 502/503 plateau from proxy TLS-handshake queueing and slow-starting upstream runtimes
preview:
Issue summary:
ScribeAI reported a persistent elevated 502/503 error rate starting 2026-03-12 02:10 UTC across multiple endpoints. Errors present as short-lived waves that plateau rather than sharp spikes — API gateway returning 502/503 while upstream runtimes report long TLS handshake queues and delayed worker star...
text:
Issue summary:
ScribeAI reported a persistent elevated 502/503 error rate starting 2026-03-12 02:10 UTC across multiple endpoints. Errors present as short-lived waves that plateau rather than sharp spikes — API gateway returning 502/503 while upstream runtimes report long TLS handshake queues and delayed worker start.

Impact:
- Customer-facing requests to ScribeAI (dedicated) experienced 10-20% error rate for ~3 hours during peak traffic windows.
- Streaming sessions intermittently failed to establish with 502s.
- Latency for successful requests increased by ~2x during plateau periods.

Environment:
- prod/us-east (dedicated pool for ScribeAI)
- Models: gptx-13b-v2 pinned variant
- Redwood stack: api-gateway -> ephemeral-proxy group -> serving-runtime (dedicated GPU pool)

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1. Generate steady small-payload traffic simulating ScribeAI client (60 rps).
2. ...

title: Cascading API-key handovers causing tenant concurrency exhaustion and intermittent 429 bursts
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a burst of 429 responses across their tenant after an automated API key rotation during a high-throughput streaming upload window. The pattern is reproducible when their client rotates keys and simultaneously spawns short-lived prefetch workers that fan out requests to multiple routes...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a burst of 429 responses across their tenant after an automated API key rotation during a high-throughput streaming upload window. The pattern is reproducible when their client rotates keys and simultaneously spawns short-lived prefetch workers that fan out requests to multiple routes.

Impact:
- LexaHealth observed ~30% request failures (429) for 7–12 minutes after an automated key rotation during a nightly data push.
- Business impact: ingestion pipeline stalled; downstream throughput SLA missed.

Steps to reproduce (provided by customer):
1) Configure two API keys (K-A primary, K-B rotated-in) with equal privileges under same tenant.
2) Client spawns 40 short-lived prefetch workers that request streaming completions and embeddings concurrently (burst over ~10s).
3) Client triggers automated rotation: start sending requests with both K-A and K-B for a...

title: Reserved pool throughput degradation caused by GPU power-capping + fragmented micro-batching
preview: Issue summary: Customer BeaconAI observed sustained p95/p99 latency regressions and intermittent 504 timeouts during peak windows on their dedicated reserved pool. Impact: Customer reports request timeouts and model responses taking 2x-4x expected time; production product is degrading for end users. Timeline: proble...
text: Issue summary: Customer BeaconAI observed sustained p95/p99 latency regressions and intermittent 504 timeouts during peak windows on their dedicated reserved pool. Impact: Customer reports request timeouts and model responses taking 2x-4x expected time; production product is degrading for end users. Timeline: problem first reported 2026-03-10 08:10 UTC, support triaged and opened this ticket. Initial hypothesis included noisy-tenant and autoscaler undershoot. This ticket captures investigation showing a combination of node-level GPU power capping (thermal/clock throttling) combined with workload micro-batching fragmentation and a conservative autoscaler min_replicas setting.

title: Intermittent timeouts and doubled latency for repeated prompts: stale prefix / KV cache misses during streamed retry path

text: Issue summary: Customer (AtlasWrite, Dedicated) reports intermittent per-request latency spikes (1.8-2.5x baseline) and occasional streaming timeouts when users resubmit identical prompts or when client SDK retries a broken stream. Impact: Several customer-facing flows (autosummarize & chat history replay) experience user-facing slowdowns and ~5-10% request failures during peak traffic windows. Environment: prod/us-east, dedicated pool id d-pool-12. Observed primarily on gptx-13b-quantized and gptx-8b-finetune. Steps to reproduce: 1) Send a multi-turn conversation with a repeated prompt token prefix. 2) Force a transient stream disconnect on the client to trigger retry logic (SDK exponential backoff). 3) Observe server-side logs and latency metrics for repeated prompt ID. Expected behavior: Repeat prompt should hit warmed prefix/KV cache and return near-baseline latency with streaming...

text: Issue summary: Customer BrightScale.ai received March invoice with a single blended line item labeled 'multi-workload blended rate' but there is no memo breakdown showing how streaming vs batch embeddings usage were apportioned or which rate tiers applied. Impact: customer cannot reconcile costs against internal chargebacks and is requesting detailed memo lines or provisional credit. Environment: production billing run for billing period 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28 covering hosted and dedicated fallbacks across us-east and eu-west. Customer says invoice total matches expectation but line-item explanation is insufficient for their finance team.

title: Partial-stream resume replay leads to duplicate external webhook side-effects for Python function-calls
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports duplicate external API calls (webhook POSTs) when using Python SDK streaming + function-calling. Behavior occurs after a network interruption causes the client to reconnect and resume a partially delivered streaming response. The resumed session appears to replay the function invocati...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports duplicate external API calls (webhook POSTs) when using Python SDK streaming + function-calling. Behavior occurs after a network interruption causes the client to reconnect and resume a partially delivered streaming response. The resumed session appears to replay the function invocation without the original idempotency token reaching the downstream webhook, resulting in side-effect duplication (duplicate invoices / duplicate task creation).

Impact:
- Customer (SlateCRM Inc.) is observing duplicate third-party webhook side-effects impacting billing and downstream task systems.
- Occurs intermittently on long-running streaming interactions where the client reconnects to resume.
- Business impact: customer flagged as P1 due to operational impact on billing pipeline.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated (customer-owned VPC) in us-east region.
- SDK: python-sd...

title: Intermittent p99 timeouts after client stream restarts due to continuous-batcher linger accumulation
preview: Issue summary: Customers (NimbleRetail dedicated tenancy) observed intermittent p99 user-facing timeouts (504 / request timeouts) concentrated after client-side stream restarts. Impact: Multiple production routes serving chat completions saw p95->p99 divergence, with p99 climbing from ~450ms baseline to 3-6s and occ...
text:
Issue summary: Customers (NimbleRetail dedicated tenancy) observed intermittent p99 user-facing timeouts (504 / request timeouts) concentrated after client-side stream restarts. Impact: Multiple production routes serving chat completions saw p95->p99 divergence, with p99 climbing from ~450ms baseline to 3-6s and occasional 504s. Affected customer reports started 2026-03-10 14:20 UTC and continued in waves.

Environment: prod (us-east), dedicated pool tenant for NimbleRetail, models: redwood-1.0-mk4 (default), fallback to gptj-14b configured.

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):
1) Client opens a streaming connection and makes a short request (~12 tokens).
2) Client intentionally restarts the stream shortly after (~300-800ms) to refresh headers/session.
3) Server continuous-batcher observed many short-lived streams that resume quickly; linger timers accumulate pending micro-batches....

title: Edge failover + auth rotation causes missing metadata envelope and trailing paragraph silence on streaming resume
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent incomplete final metadata and a silent trailing paragraph when their websocket or SSE stream resumes after an edge failover/auth cookie rotation. The response body contains the content tokens but the final metadata envelope (terminal JSON object containing stream-level fi...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent incomplete final metadata and a silent trailing paragraph when their websocket or SSE stream resumes after an edge failover/auth cookie rotation. The response body contains the content tokens but the final metadata envelope (terminal JSON object containing stream-level fields like 'id'/'usage'/'choices' metadata) is sometimes missing or cut and the last paragraph is not emitted, causing the client SDK to throw a parse error or appear to hang.

Impact:
- Several Inkwell Media users see a parse error or a UI spinner after a reconnect; last paragraph (summary) often never appears.
- Occurs ~0.5-1% of long-running streaming sessions for this customer during peak traffic.
- Affects critical editor flows where the final paragraph contains a license blurb.

Environment:
- Customer: Inkwell Media (dedicated account)
- Region: us-east
- Model: redwo...

title: Streaming tool/function output tokens billed on final invoice despite console estimate showing zero
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that streaming requests which include function/tool calls produced console estimates showing 0 tool-output tokens (console estimate column), but the finalized invoice contains token charges attributed to the tool outputs. Impact: LexiHealth reports a $9,200 unexpected charge on their ...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reports that streaming requests which include function/tool calls produced console estimates showing 0 tool-output tokens (console estimate column), but the finalized invoice contains token charges attributed to the tool outputs. Impact: LexiHealth reports a $9,200 unexpected charge on their monthly invoice tied to streamed sessions that used function calls for structured data retrieval. Environment: Production, us-east-1, enterprise account with reserved capacity and hosted fallback enabled. This affects streaming chat endpoints where the model returns tool invocations (function calls) and the tool outputs are streamed back as separate chunks.

Expected behavior: Console and estimate API should include a consistent token estimate for tool outputs (either zero if non-billable or the same token estimate used for invoice attribution). Final invoice should not int...

title: Halogen Energy — fanout stall causing streaming silence, duplicate responses, and invoice exposure (post-incident follow-up)
preview: Issue summary: During a high-concurrency scheduled batch for Halogen Energy (enterprise, prod us-east), the request fanout path experienced a sustained stall resulting in partial streaming responses followed by duplicate completions. Impact: 11% of in-flight user sessions experienced either (a) a streaming silence l...
text: Issue summary: During a high-concurrency scheduled batch for Halogen Energy (enterprise, prod us-east), the request fanout path experienced a sustained stall resulting in partial streaming responses followed by duplicate completions. Impact: 11% of in-flight user sessions experienced either (a) a streaming silence lasting 6–18s before a replayed completion, or (b) duplicate completions delivered after client-side retry. Billing exposure: duplicate tokens were recorded for 0.8% of requests between 2026-03-10T09:42Z and 2026-03-10T10:26Z. User-visible effect: client-facing SDKs reported truncated transcripts and duplicated final messages; several Halogen automated workflows misinterpreted duplicates and triggered payment processing retrials. Environment: production, us-east, Dedicated pool: ded-pool-us-east-3, routing policy: capacity-first with multipath fanout to 3 backends. Time wind...

title: session-kv-lease-sticky-causing-resumed-conversation-p99-latency-spike
preview: Issue summary: ClearHealth reported a series of resumed conversational requests (user resumes session after short disconnect) that observe elevated tail latency (p99 ~4.2s) and occasional timeouts, compared to normal p99 ~250ms. This occurs after sessions have been inactive for ~30-90s. Impact: user-facing latency s...
text:
Issue summary: ClearHealth reported a series of resumed conversational requests (user resumes session after short disconnect) that observe elevated tail latency (p99 ~4.2s) and occasional timeouts, compared to normal p99 ~250ms. This occurs after sessions have been inactive for ~30-90s. Impact: user-facing latency spikes and a small number of 504 timeouts causing degraded UX in production. Behavior is reproducible for certain session IDs and high-frequency resume patterns.

Context: This ticket was opened by Support after customer escalation. Initial triage indicates the serving runtime enters a blocking cold-path when a session key is considered "leased" by another backend shard due to a lease state that does not expire promptly. The behavior started after the kvcache lease change in release v2.18.

preview: Issue summary: Customer requests a one-off delta export of their tenant activity as a CSV with strict UTC timestamp normalization, a rotating MD5 manifest for each batch file, and a machine-verifiable 'proof-of-exclusion' artifact listing records deleted since the last snapshot. text:
Issue summary: Customer requests a one-off delta export of their tenant activity as a CSV with strict UTC timestamp normalization, a rotating MD5 manifest for each batch file, and a machine-verifiable 'proof-of-exclusion' artifact listing records deleted since the last snapshot. They need the package for a regulatory evidence submission and require deterministic ordering and replayability.

Requirements: (1) CSV delta with canonical UTC timestamps and ISO8601 formatting; (2) each batch file accompanied by an MD5 manifest entry and a signed manifest file (HMAC-SHA256 with their provided per-tenant key); (3) a separate "proof-of-exclusion" file: a compact list of deleted record IDs with per-ID deletion-tombstone hashes and a top-level Merkle-root signed by Redwood to prove non-inclusion in prior snapshots; (4) resume tokens and offsets for replay; (5) timezone normalization mapping note...

title: Tool acknowledgement lost after client-side backpressure causing ambiguous replay and side-effect uncertainty
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that when their client applies aggressive client-side backpressure (pause/resume on the streaming socket) while the SDK/edge is in the middle of a function/tool call, the tool acknowledgement gets lost. Impact: downstream systems observe either duplicate side-effects after replay or n...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reports that when their client applies aggressive client-side backpressure (pause/resume on the streaming socket) while the SDK/edge is in the middle of a function/tool call, the tool acknowledgement gets lost. Impact: downstream systems observe either duplicate side-effects after replay or never-observed confirmations, leading to billing ambiguity and customer data inconsistencies.

Environment: Prod, dedicated deployment for Nimbus Health in us-east. Python SDK 1.8.2 used from a serverless worker behind an in-house proxy.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Start long-lived streaming session via Python SDK to call external tool via function-calling feature.
2) Trigger a tool execution that emits a confirmation webhook synchronously from the tool handler.
3) Simulate client backpressure by pausing the TCP read loop for ~500-1500ms intermittently (server in CPU-constrained...

Impact: customer requests experience 3x p99 increase and b...
text: Issue summary: During the customer's recurring midday window we observed a sustained p95/p99 throughput degradation for NovaDocs' reserved pool that coincided with a large number of short-lived tenant connections spreading across many slots (tenant scatter). Impact: customer requests experience 3x p99 increase and burst 429/RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED for batched routes, causing downstream timeouts for user-facing documents ingestion pipelines. Environment: production reserved pool (dedicated) in us-east. Observed starting 2026-03-10 11:40 UTC. Steps to reproduce: 1) Replay customer traffic (small, high-concurrency short-streams) into reserved pool using synthetic tenant generator 2) Observe slot churn and repeated small allocations 3) Watch autoscaler not react quickly due to many ephemeral allocations and scheduler fragmentation Observed behavior: rapid slot attach/detach, high scheduler RPC...

title: Intermittent websocket half-close after corporate TLS rekey -> trailing 'done' envelope never arrives
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent websocket sessions that appear to close from the server side after a TLS rekey performed by their corporate TLS-inspecting proxy. The stream is truncated: the final JSON envelope with status/done metadata is never received, leaving the client waiting indefinitely. Impact:...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent websocket sessions that appear to close from the server side after a TLS rekey performed by their corporate TLS-inspecting proxy. The stream is truncated: the final JSON envelope with status/done metadata is never received, leaving the client waiting indefinitely. Impact: Multiple production users at Acme AI Labs experience truncated generations and must reissue requests; affects business-critical inference pipelines. Observed first at ~2026-03-09 22:15 PST and recurred several times. Environment: prod cluster behind ALB -> CloudFront -> corporate TLS-inspection -> customer client. No code changes on Redwood side around times of first occurrence.

Issue summary:
Customers see a truncated final output and missing attachment URL when the client performs an SSE stream that upgrades to WebSocket mid-response. The last few tokens (often the attachment link or final punctuation) are dropped and the stream terminates without the terminal metadata envelope.

prod - us-west, api gateway behind ALB -> edge proxy cluster -> serving-runtime pool. Customer using SDK-js v1.2.4 with automatic SSE-to-WS upgrade enabled.

title: Progress-callbacks + client-side retry causing unacknowledged tool executions and billing confusion
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer uses the TypeScript SDK in a serverless worker to issue long-running orchestrations that include multiple tool/function calls per user request. They've enabled progress callbacks (per-action progress events) so their frontend can display live status. Recently they observed duplicate billing f...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer uses the TypeScript SDK in a serverless worker to issue long-running orchestrations that include multiple tool/function calls per user request. They've enabled progress callbacks (per-action progress events) so their frontend can display live status. Recently they observed duplicate billing for tool executions: the backend logs show the tool run completed, but the client timed out waiting for the final progress acknowledgement and retried the whole orchestration, which triggered the tooling to re-execute. The customer is requesting guidance on how to avoid unverified double-executions and how to reconcile charges.

Impact:
- Customer reports 2-8% increase in tooling cost for a specific heavy workflow.
- UI shows transient "stuck" state when progress callbacks are lost; sometimes user retries from browser which results in duplicate downstream effects (e.g., dupl...

title: IdP-initiated SLO probe truncates session cookie domain causing re-auth loops and 401s in private VPC
preview: Issue summary: After configuring IdP-initiated SSO for Nimbus Health's Private deployment, periodic IdP single-logout (SLO) probes from their vendor (AuthWave) are observed to cause the Redwood ingress to rewrite the session cookie domain by truncating tenant namespace suffixes. Impact: End-users and service account...
text: Issue summary: After configuring IdP-initiated SSO for Nimbus Health's Private deployment, periodic IdP single-logout (SLO) probes from their vendor (AuthWave) are observed to cause the Redwood ingress to rewrite the session cookie domain by truncating tenant namespace suffixes. Impact: End-users and service accounts intermittently hit 401/reauth loops across the Redwood admin and ingest APIs. Expected: SLO probes should not alter or truncate session cookie domain/path; logout events should be processed without breaking other sessions. Environment details: Private control plane deployed into customer's VPC using edge-proxy v2.1.7, ingress-proxy v3.4.0, auth-bridge v1.9. Deployed certs: internal mTLS + customer-managed IdP metadata via SAML. Observed window: recurring since 2026-03-09 02:00 UTC, roughly every 6–8 hours when IdP health probes run.

title: Layered 429s observed during rolling API token swapback causing throughput collapse
preview: Issue summary: Customer (Acme Analytics, dedicated) reported sudden and sustained 429s beginning ~2026-03-10T14:20Z that coincided with a scheduled rolling API key rotation. Impact: interactive chat and batch embed pipelines degraded; sustained 30-60% request rejection for 20 minutes and client-side retry storms. En...
text:
Issue summary: Customer (Acme Analytics, dedicated) reported sudden and sustained 429s beginning ~2026-03-10T14:20Z that coincided with a scheduled rolling API key rotation. Impact: interactive chat and batch embed pipelines degraded; sustained 30-60% request rejection for 20 minutes and client-side retry storms. Environment: prod-us-west (us-west-2), dedicated pool id dp-88. Customer reports that their orchestrator performs aggressive short retries and they observed token swaps in their logs.

Initial hypothesis: rolling credential refresh led to swapback behavior where some ingress proxies temporarily presented rotated keys while backend accounting still counted against the old token windows. This created token-window misalignment and effective fanout of short-lived keys across pooled connections, exhausting tenant burst buckets and triggering layered 429s.

Why ticket created: cust...
